mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals expanding household income and consumption demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month increases in PAYEMS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.0%, restrictive monetary policy raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuation multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.0%, subsequent 60-day SPX forward returns are positive in greater than 55% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) confirms economy-wide demand expansion rather than sector-specific noise, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint month-over-month increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 20-day SPX returns in at least 60% of joint-signal instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Under accommodative monetary policy, month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) reflect healthy demand reflation that supports revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 2.0%, positive month-over-month CPIAUCSL changes are followed by negative 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) indicates manufacturing utilization gains and operating leverage, which boosts earnings forecasts and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO increases are not followed by significantly positive 20-day SPX returns (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative 0.3 percentage point rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) over three months signals deteriorating labor-market conditions and recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE rises more than 0.3pp in 3 months are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in greater than 60% of cases over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above its two-year trend indicates aggregate demand outperformance, leading to upward earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth above its 2-year rolling median does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 55% of quarters over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) contracts month-over-month by more than 1%, it signals acute manufacturing weakness that spills into earnings guidance cuts and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Month-over-month INDPRO declines exceeding 1% are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 3-year window.
